hook: Your voice is part of the signal, not just a delivery system for words.
body: Cadence, volume, and breath shape whether you sound calm, credible, rushed, or needy.

hook: Speaking too fast can turn good content into anxious content without changing a single sentence.
body: Vocal steadiness is a social cue that tells people your body can tolerate the moment.

body: A shorter sentence spoken clearly often lands harder than a longer sentence spoken while trying not to be interrupted.

body: Record your voice in casual speech and listen for speed, trailing endings, and where you stop breathing.
